findings.resolution: CP8 accepted these rows after the cleanroom input supplied the generic SpellBaseArmorClassEffectRouteSubject connector; target admission, armored rejection, and duration expiry now route through observed BattleEntrypointTrace route events.
findings.summary: Mage Armor discovery, armored rejection, and duration expiry are not accepted reducer routes.

findings.resolution: Added generic ReactionSpell fills for armor-class interruption and failed-save damage facts, then routed accepted rows through production discovery from explicit reaction-window substrates; production code does not dispatch on selected spell identity.
findings.summary: Reaction branches could have been routed by selected spell name.

rounds.summary: Checked RAW/QNT traceability, domain language, architecture/connascence, adapter quarantine, state ownership, and report honesty after FU01E route implementation.

Convergence pass after keeping CP7-D Mage Armor target-admission and active-effect lifecycle rows blocked and confirming accepted AC/reaction rows use reducer entrypoint route evidence, with ReactionSpell rows discovered from production reaction-window state; no reasonable findings remain.

CP8 convergence pass after accepting exactly three Mage Armor rows through the generic SpellBaseArmorClassEffectRouteSubject connector. Production reducer code uses generic target-admission, active-effect, turn-boundary, and Armor Class owner facts; selected Mage Armor identity remains quarantined to adapter/evidence row names.

Follow-up review accepted the preferred fix for the pre-existing Mage Armor base projection row: replay now matches the refreshed generic connector sequence of target admission, active-effect no-fill projection, and Armor Class no-fill projection. ArmorClassProjection is intentionally a state-read/no-op BattleArmorClassOwner boundary, but its live check is narrowed to post-action states with BattleState.action_available=false so fresh standalone projection requests remain stale. CP8 accounting remains exactly three newly accepted rows.
